title: Platform Channels
objectives: Call native code from Dart and back

sections.markdown: Platform channels are the bridge between Dart and native (Kotlin/Java, Swift/Obj-C) code. Messages are serialized and passed asynchronously across the channel by name.

rows: Call a native method, get a result
Stream events from native to Dart (sensors, location)

sections.code:
const ch = MethodChannel('app/battery');
final level = await ch.invokeMethod<int>('getBatteryLevel');

sections.markdown: Channels are asynchronous and use a standard message codec (numbers, strings, lists, maps, bytes). Heavy/synchronous data (lots of bytes) is better moved via FFI or shared memory than serialized over a channel each call.

Doing long native work on the platform main thread (blocks the UI), or assuming channel calls are synchronous — they're always async. Use a background thread/dispatcher natively and return via the result callback.
Wrap channels in a typed Dart API (a plugin) so the rest of the app doesn't touch raw invokeMethod. Match method names and argument shapes exactly on both sides.

flashcards.back: Calling native platform code from Dart (and streaming back), via named, serialized async messages.
MethodChannel for request/response calls; EventChannel for a stream of native events.

flashcards.front: Are channel calls sync or async?
flashcards.back: Always asynchronous; results come back via Futures/streams.

quiz.q: To stream sensor events from native to Dart, use…
quiz.explain: EventChannel exposes a native stream to Dart.
FFI avoids per-call serialization overhead for heavy data.

challenges.text: Call a native method that returns the battery level.
Stream accelerometer events to Dart via EventChannel.
Run heavy native work off the main thread and return a result.
Wrap a native SDK in a typed Flutter plugin with method + event channels.

interviewQuestions: What are platform channels and how do they work?
Are channel calls sync or async?
How do you avoid blocking the UI from native work?
When use FFI instead of a channel?
